questions.question: An airfield 2400ft. AMSL has a QFE of 1008. The QNH is likely to be:
LCLK O40400 13005KT 7000 HZ SCT035 SCT045 14/12 Q1016 NOSIG

options.a: There is haze at Larnaca with a cloud base of 7000ft.
options.b: The atmosphere at Larnaca is very dry and no significant change is forecast.
options.c: There is haze at Larnaca with a visibility of 7000 meters.
options.d: There are strong winds at Larnaca with stratocumulus at 3500 feet and 4500 feet.

questions.question: The ICAO published International Standard Atmosphere (ISA) is:

options.a: 1013.2 hPa, +58 degrees F, density 1225 grams per cubic metre.
options.b: 1013.3 hPa, +13 degrees C, density 1235.2 grams per cubic metre.
options.c: 1013 0 hPa, +15 degrees F, density 1325 0 grams per cubic metre.
options.d: 1013.2 hPa, +15 degrees C, density 1225 grams per cubic metre.

questions.question: If you stand with your back to the wind in the Northern Hemisphere the high pressure will be:

options.a: On your left.
options.c: In front of you.
options.d: Behind you.

questions.question: Coriolis force deflects air moving south in the Northern Hemisphere:

options.a: Upwards.
options.c: To the left.
options.d: Coriolis force has no effect when air is moving south in the northern hemisphere.

questions.question: Flying near Troodos you see a long, thin lens-shaped cloud over the top of the mountains and another cloud downwind of the crest. You should anticipate the following conditions downwind of the mountains:

options.a: Smooth, descending air.
options.c: Turbulent rising air.
options.d: Turbulent descending air.

questions.question: Thunderstorms can occur in which of the following cases:

options.a: Along a cold front.
options.b: On a hot summer day with stable air mass.
options.c: During very cold and dry conditions.
options.d: Over the mountains with very stable air mass.

questions.question: An aircraft is flying towards the coast from the sea on a QNH of 1030 and the altimeter shows 4000ft. If the pilot sets 1013 what pressure altitude will the altimeter show?

An aircraft departs airfield A which is 700 feet above mean sea level and has a QNH of 1010 which is set on the altimeter. The aircraft flies towards low pressure and the altimeter setting is not changed. A visual landing is made at airfield B which is 1400 feet above mean sea level. What will the altimeter read at B if the subscale setting remains unchanged and the QNH at B 995?
Large super cooled water droplets striking a cold airframe are likely to form:

options.a: Rime ice.
options.c: Frost
options.d: No ice will form as the water is too cold.

questions.question: CAVOK means:

options.a: Visibility 10km or more, no cloud below 5000ft, no cumulonimbus and no significant weather in the vicinity of the aerodrome.
options.b: Visibility unlimited, no cloud, no significant weather expected for 6 hours.
options.c: Visibility not affected by mist or haze, no cloud below 10000ft, wind below 10kts.
options.d: Visibility 20km, no cloud below 5000ft, no cumulonimbus or significant weather in the vicinity of the aerodrome, wind below 10 Kts

questions.question: Ci, Cs, As, Ns, St. This sequence of clouds indicates the passage of a:

options.a: Cold front.
options.c: Warm front
options.d: Warm occlusion

questions.question: Relative humidity is:

options.a: The ratio of water vapour in the air relative to what it can hold at 1013 hPa.
options.b: The ratio of water in the air relative to what it could hold at 15 degrees C.
options.c: The ratio of water vapour in the air relative to what it could hold at a particular temperature and pressure.
options.d: The ratio of water in the air relative to what it could hold at 105 degrees C.

questions.question: A temperature group of 28/24 in a METAR means:

options.a: The temperature is 28 degrees C at the time of the report and expected to be 24 degrees at the end of the reporting period.
options.b: The temperature is 28 degrees C and the dewpoint is 24.
options.c: The dewpoint is 28 degrees C and the temperature is 24.
options.d: The temperature is now 28 degrees C and expected to fall to 24 degrees C night.

questions.question: On a cross country flight you experience increasing left drift. Which statement is likely to be correct?

options.a: The QNH is changing to a lower value.
options.c: The QNH is changing to a higher value.
options.d: It shouldn't be a change in the QNH as it's just a windy day.

questions.question: The passage of a cold front is indicated by:

options.a: A rise in dewpoint, a fall in temperature and the wind backing.
options.b: A fall in dewpoint, a fall in temperature and the wind veering.
options.c: A rise in dewpoint, a fall in temperature and the wind veering.
options.d: A fall in dewpoint, a rise in temperature and the wind backing.

questions.question: An aircraft with a constant altimeter setting of 1009 hPa flies at a constant indicated altitude from A (QNH 1009) to B (QNH 1019). When overhead B the indicated altitude will be:

options.a: Higher or lower depending on the elevation of B.
options.c: 1200ft above B.
options.d: Higher than actual altitude.

questions.question: If the wind at altitude is 240/35, the most likely wind on the ground is:
The environmental lapse rate is observed to be 2.5 degrees C / 1000 feet. Which of the following statements is correct?

options.a: A dry parcel of air would be stable if it was forced to rise.
options.b: A dry parcel of air would be unstable if it was forced to rise.
options.c: A saturated parcel of air would be stable if it was forced to rise.
options.d: A saturated parcel of air would dry if it was forced to rise.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct?

options.a: The wind blowing anticlockwise round a depression in the northern hemisphere is deflected inwards towards the low pressure by the net effect of Coriolis and pressure gradient forces.
options.b: The wind blowing anticlockwise round a depression in the northern hemisphere is deflected outwards away from the low pressure by the net effect of Coriolis and pressure gradient forces.
options.c: The wind blowing clockwise round an anticyclone in the northern hemisphere the wind speed will be less than in a depression with the same isobar spacing.
options.d: The wind blowing clockwise round an anticyclone in the northern Hemisphere is deflected inwards by the gradient wind.

questions.question: The temperature at 11000 feet AMSL is forecast to be minus 5 degrees C. Compared to the International Standard Atmosphere (ISA), this is:

options.a: ISA -2 degrees C.
options.c: ISA -7 degrees C.
options.d: ISA +5 degrees C.

questions.question: Air density is proportional to:

options.a: Temperature and inversely proportional to pressure.
options.b: Pressure and inversely proportional to temperature.
options.c: Temperature and humidity.
options.d: Pressure and relative humidity.

questions.question: On a cross country flight, you experience increasing left drift. Which statement is likely to be correct?

options.a: The QNH is rising to a higher value.
options.c: The QNH is falling to a lower value.
options.d: The QFE is unchanged.

questions.question: Which of these statements is not true of Advection fog:

options.a: It is caused by warm moist air flowing over a cold surface.
options.c: Light winds cause advection fog to thicken.
options.d: It occurs most frequently during the passage of a frontal system.

questions.question: All of these types of contamination are dangerous, which is likely to be the greatest hazard to light aircraft:

options.a: Rime ice.
options.c: Clear ice.
options.d: Cloudy ice.

questions.question: Cloud bases in TAFS and METARS are given in heights:

options.a: Based on official aerodrome level.
options.c: Based on QNH.
options.d: Above the published height of the threshold of the runway in use at the time of the observation.

questions.question: The Katabatic wind:

options.a: Is weaker than the Anabatic wind.
options.b: Is stronger than the Anabatic wind.
options.c: Is approximately the same speed as the Anabatic wind
options.d: Never exceeds 15 knots.

questions.question: The rate of fall of pressure with increasing altitude is:

options.a: Greater in cold air than warm air
options.b: Greater in warm air than cold air.
options.c: Is variable with increasing height.
options.d: Is the same for cold and warm air.

questions.question: Air density will be low when:

options.a: Pressure and temperature are low.
options.c: Pressure is low and temperature is high.
options.d: Pressure is high and temperature is low

questions.question: The temperature at 10000 feet above mean sea level in the International Standard Atmosphere should be:

options.a: -5 degrees C.
options.c: -15 degrees C
options.d: -20 degrees C.

questions.question: The symbol on a weather chart means: (S-curve symbol)

options.a: Rain showers.
options.c: Visibility reduced by smoke.
options.d: Severe sand or dust haze.

questions.question: The symbol on a weather chart means: (triangular teeth on line symbol)

options.a: A cold front.
options.c: An occluded front.
options.d: A mountain wave.

questions.question: What is the dewpoint temperature?

options.a: The temperature below which dew cannot form.
options.b: The temperature at which air becomes saturated if cooled at constant pressure.
options.c: The temperature at which air can support more water vapour.
options.d: The temperature above which the relative humidity is greater than 100 %.

questions.question: The decode of the following TAF is: LCLK 2008 12010KT 9999 DZ SCTO10TCU TEMPO 2023 5000 BKN010+SHRAGR

options.a: Larnaca, wind 200/8 Kts to 120/10kts, 10+n.mls visibility, stratocumulus at 10000ft. broken cumulus at 2000ft., temporarily, between 2000UTC and 2300UTC, broken cumulonimbus and showers of rain reaching to ground level.
options.b: Larnaca, wind 200/8kts to 120/10kts 10+n.mls visibility, drizzle, scattered at base 1000ft.temporarily down to 100ft.,broken cumulonimbus at 2000ft., temporarily from 2000UTC to 2300UTC, broken cumulonimbus and showers of rain, heavy at times.
options.c: Larnaca, 2000UTC to 0800UTC, wind 120 degrees magnetic at 10 mph, visibility 10 Nm., drizzle, cloud 3-4 oktas towering cumulus base 1000 feet, 5-7 oktas of cloud at 2000 feet temporarily, between 2000UTC and 2300UTC visibility 5000 metres with 5-7 oktas cumulonimbus at 1000ft with heavy rain showers and hail.
options.d: Larnaca, 0000UTC-0800UTC, wind 120 degrees true at 10kts. visibility greater than 10 kilometres, drizzle, cloud 3-4 oktas towering cumulus base 1000ft., 5-7 other cloud, base 2000ft.; temporarily between 2000UTC and 2300UTC, visibility reduced to 5000 meters, 5-7 oktas cumulonimbus base 1000ft. with heavy showers and hail.

questions.question: The ICAO Standard Atmosphere assumes a pressure of 1013 hPa and a temperature of +15 degrees C at mean sea level. What are the pressure and temperature values at a pressure altitude of 5000 feet?

options.a: 826 hPa and +2 degrees C.
options.c: 799 hPa and – 1 degree C.
options.d: 805 hPa and +1 degree C.

questions.question: Winds that blow round an anticyclone (high pressure) in the northern hemisphere are shown on a low level weather chart as blowing in:

options.a: A clockwise direction.
options.c: A clockwise direction if it is a warm Airmass.
options.d: An anticlockwise direction if it is a cold Airmass.

questions.question: Thickening Cu and Cb, temperature falling, dewpoint falling, pressure falling, wind increasing, heavy rain, rapid wind veer, pressure rise, thinning Cu. This sequence indicates the passage of a:

options.a: Cold occlusion.
options.c: Warm Front
options.d: Warm occlusion.

questions.question: You are flying in a cold sector at or below the freezing level. Rain is falling from warmer air from an overlying warm front. What type of icing are you likely to get?

options.a: Carburettor Ice.
options.c: Rime Ice
options.d: No ice will form.

questions.question: Sea breezes and land breezes develop along coastal areas, which of these statements is correct:

options.a: The vertical extent of sea breezes is up to 5000 feet
options.b: The horizontal motion of air in a land breeze is called convection.
options.c: Sea breezes form when the land heats up during the day.
options.d: Land breezes form when the sea cools down in winter.

questions.question: Lenticularis clouds over mountain ranges indicate to a light aircraft, approaching from downwind, the likely presence of:

options.a: Severe turbulence especially on the leeward side with the presence of rotor clouds.
options.b: Strong Katabatic down currents and turbulence after the mountains are crossed.
options.c: Strong down currents immediately before the tops of the mountains and strong up currents immediately after crossing.
options.d: No turbulence or up/down currents are likely as lenticular clouds form at too high an altitude above mountains to have an effect on an aircraft.
